name: context7-auto-research
description: Automatically fetch latest library/framework documentation for Claude Code via Context7 API
capabilities: Fetch current documentation for popular libraries and frameworks via Context7 API
Auto-trigger documentation lookups during coding conversations
Support for React, Next.js, Prisma, and other popular libraries
Optional API key configuration for increased rate limits
Seamless integration with Claude Code workflow
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged external_commands and network patterns, but manual review confirms all findings are false positives. The external_commands finding references an npm installation command in documentation. The network finding points to a GitHub URL reference in markdown. No actual code execution or network requests exist in the skill files. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Stop relying on outdated documentation in your AI training data. This skill connects Claude Code to the Context7 API for real-time access to current library and framework documentation.
limitations: Requires Context7 API access for optimal functionality
Limited to libraries indexed by the Context7 service
Rate limits apply without API key configuration
Documentation quality depends on Context7 indexing accuracy

useCases.targetUser: Frontend developers using modern React ecosystems
useCases.title: Frontend developer working with latest React features
useCases.description: Get up-to-date information about React hooks, server components, and new API changes that may not be in your model training data

useCases.targetUser: Backend developers working with evolving dependencies
useCases.title: Backend engineer integrating new library versions
useCases.description: Fetch accurate documentation for recently released library versions to ensure code compatibility and best practices

useCases.targetUser: Full-stack developers managing diverse tech stacks
useCases.title: Full-stack developer exploring unfamiliar frameworks
useCases.description: Quickly access official documentation patterns when working across multiple technology stacks

promptTemplates.title: Basic library lookup
promptTemplates.prompt: Look up the current documentation for [library name] and explain how to use [specific feature]
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick reference for a known library

promptTemplates.title: Compare library versions
promptTemplates.prompt: Fetch the latest docs for [library] and explain what changed in version [X] compared to version [Y]

promptTemplates.title: Integration pattern research
promptTemplates.prompt: Research how to integrate [library A] with [library B] using current best practices from their documentation

promptTemplates.title: API reference deep-dive
promptTemplates.prompt: Fetch complete API documentation for [specific module or class] including all methods, parameters, and return types
promptTemplates.scenario: Detailed implementation work requiring full API knowledge

bestPractices: Configure your Context7 API key for higher rate limits and reliable access
Use specific library names and versions when requesting documentation
Let the skill auto-trigger when you mention library names in conversation
antiPatterns: Do not rely on this skill for security-critical documentation without verification
Avoid using without API key for production work due to rate limiting
Do not use for libraries not indexed by Context7 without manual verification

faq.question: Do I need a Context7 API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No, but configuring an API key provides higher rate limits and more reliable access. The skill works with default rate limits without a key.

faq.question: Which libraries are supported?
faq.answer: Context7 indexes popular libraries and frameworks including React, Next.js, Prisma, Vue, Svelte, and many others. Check the Context7 documentation for the complete list.

faq.question: How do I configure the API key?
faq.answer: Set the Context7 API key via environment variables as described in the Context7 documentation or the skill GitHub repository.

faq.question: Can this skill access my code or files?
faq.answer: No, this skill only fetches documentation from the Context7 API. It does not read your local files or send your code to external services.

faq.question: What happens if Context7 API is down?
faq.answer: The skill will fall back to Claude built-in knowledge. You will receive documentation based on the model training data instead of live API results.

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with all Claude Code versions?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It integrates seamlessly through the skills system.
